message:
fix(exec): add WS ping/pong heartbeat to prevent zombie goroutines (#6891) (#6892)

When a WebSocket client disconnects without a clean close frame (hard
power failure, network drop), the goroutine reading from the WS blocks
forever on ReadMessage, leaking the exec goroutine and its SPDY stream.

Add a ping/pong heartbeat mechanism:
- Server sends a WebSocket ping every 30s
- Pong handler resets the read deadline to 45s on each reply
- If pong is not received in time, ReadMessage returns a deadline error,
  the read goroutine exits, and execCancel() fires to tear down the
  SPDY stream
- If the ping write itself fails, execCancel() is called immediately

This ensures half-open connections are detected and cleaned up within
~45 seconds instead of leaking indefinitely.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
